summary: Matched three functions in src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c: un_8031BB94, un_8031BBF4, and un_8031C354. The PR also updated tydisplay.h prototypes for two recovered functions. Evidence includes author-stated 100% fuzzy match, clean ninja build, and bot report of three new 100% matches.

key_files.role: Implemented three previously undecompiled functions, added asset/string lookup tables, loop filtering logic, PAD_STACK(8), and an extern for un_804D5AC0.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c added 129 deleted 3 hunks 3
raw_diff: implementations for un_8031BB94, un_8031BBF4, un_8031C354
pr_body: Decompile un_8031BB94, un_8031BBF4, un_8031C354 — 100% match
bot_report: 3 new matches in main/melee/ty/tydisplay

key_files.role: Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for un_8031BB94 and un_8031BBF4 with recovered signatures.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ty/tydisplay.h added 2 deleted 2 hunks 1
raw_diff: un_8031BB94 changed to char* un_8031BB94(s8); un_8031BBF4 changed to s32 un_8031BBF4(s8)

what_changed.text: un_8031BB94 was implemented as a signed-byte-indexed table returning animation joint/resource name strings, with idx == -1 normalized to 0 and un_804D5AC0 used for several entries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: char* un_8031BB94(s8 idx)
raw_diff: table entries ToyDspMycharaCmA_Top_matanim_joint through ToyDspItemE_Top_matanim_joint and un_804D5AC0

what_changed.text: un_8031BBF4 was implemented as a signed-byte-indexed table returning .dat asset filenames cast to s32, with arg0 == -1 normalized to 0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: s32 un_8031BBF4(s8 arg0)
raw_diff: table entries TyQuesD.dat, TyMycCmA.dat, TyMapA.dat, TyStandD.dat, etc.

what_changed.text: un_8031C354 was implemented as a loop over ids 0..0x124 that skips invalid/self/nonmatching entries, appends matching ids to a caller buffer, and stops when count reaches max.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: s32 un_8031C354(s32 id, s32 (*buf)[], s32 max, s32 kind)
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 0x125; i++) with continue filters and break on count >= max

what_changed.text: Header prototypes were recovered for un_8031BB94 and un_8031BBF4, replacing unknown return/parameter macros.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tydisplay.h UNK_RET declarations replaced

smart_moves.text: The final diff used early continue statements inside the un_8031C354 loop to express skip conditions clearly; this corresponds to reviewer feedback replacing a goto-style skip.
evidence_refs: review_comment PsiLupan: `continue;` on hunk showing if (i == id) { goto next; }
raw_diff: final un_8031C354 uses continue for each skip condition

smart_moves.text: Recovered function signatures were reflected in the owning header instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS after body recovery.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/ty/tydisplay.h prototype updates

smart_moves.text: The PR kept ordinary asset/resource strings inline in the lookup tables rather than promoting every string to new global data symbols; only existing un_804D5AC0 was referenced as an extern.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_8031BB94 inline ToyDsp... strings plus un_804D5AC0
raw_diff: un_8031BBF4 inline Ty... .dat strings

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a signature, update the corresponding header prototype at the same time.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_8031BB94 and un_8031BBF4 declarations changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures

decomp_lessons.lesson: For loop filters, prefer direct continue statements over generated-looking goto/label skip patterns when the control flow is equivalent and review feedback supports it.
evidence_refs: review_comment PsiLupan: `continue;`
raw_diff: un_8031C354 final loop uses continue for skip conditions

decomp_lessons.lesson: Lookup-table functions may match using local stack arrays of inline string literals when the function is a simple index-to-asset-name mapper; avoid turning such literals into new globals without ownership evidence.

raw_diff: local char* table[] in un_8031BBF4

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use continue instead of goto skip labels in recovered filter loops when equivalent
